Post Comment
Collapsible Distillery
QuoteReply
# Apr 22 2018 at 5:08 AM Rating: Decent
Conelder
12 posts
There's not enough room to make a water flask in a collapsible distillery. The collapsible distillery only provides for four slots while it is requires four pods of water and a bottle to make a water flask.
____________________________
01000011 01101111 01101110 01100101 01101100 01100100 01100101 01110010 00100000 01101111 01100110 00100000 01010100 01100101 01110010 01101111 01101011 01101011 01100001 01110010
▲ Page top
Collapsible Distillery
QuoteReply
# Apr 28 2018 at 3:08 PM Rating: Excellent
Gidono
View User Forum
Moderator
6,835 posts
The One and Only Conelder wrote:
There's not enough room to make a water flask in a collapsible distillery. The collapsible distillery only provides for four slots while it is requires four pods of water and a bottle to make a water flask.


Thanks, that is fixed. As soon as I clicked edit on the recipe it gave me the error for that container and one other. The recipe was added before programming was put in place to check for recipes with containers that are too small for it.
____________________________

Maximum stack size is 100, not 1000.

Incorrect stack size in description
QuoteReply
# Dec 12 2017 at 12:53 PM Rating: Decent
Philliden
Scholar
200 posts
Gidono wrote:
Philliden wrote:
Maximum stack size is 100, not 1000.


This item was verified on 2017-11-12 22:27:05. Where did you get your water flasks from that say they only stack to 100?

My mistake. I purchased a stack using "shift" and was only given 100 and presumed they gave you as many as the largest stack value. Sorry.

As an aside, what does the value in parentheses represent in Drink Duration: Drink(15)? Is that the frequency in minutes of food/drink consumption?

Edited, Dec 12th 2017 12:56pm by Philliden

Bought in Neriak
QuoteReply
# Dec 16 2015 at 1:58 PM Rating: Good
Gidono
View User Forum
Moderator
6,835 posts
On Phinigel server, this is also sold by

a spell research merchant

in Neriak Commons. Location is 133, -977, -25 in the north part of the zone where all the casters guild masters are.

Cost with indifferent faction is 1s 2cp.

# Feb 16 2005 at 5:52 PM Rating: Decent

Scholar
40 posts
Just to add to that list.. they also drop off of gnoll pups in qeynos and everfrost on occasion. As well as off of the ice goblin whelps in everfrost..
They are not a common drop, but they do drop off both of these mobs occasionally.

Hide/Show
QuoteReply
# Jul 28 2003 at 12:32 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591788592110
77 posts
Great Option!

Would be nice to preset Crafted, Drops, Vendors, Quests, and Recipes hide/show options on my account but a great change just as it has been setup!!

Maybe the Chat following items could be toggled also. When I print an item, I sometimes get 12 pages of chat when all I wanted was the item information for gameplay.

Thanx.
▲ Page top
RE: Hide/Show
QuoteReply
# Jul 15 2005 at 9:54 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591785075777
148 posts
you could always highlight what you want and select print selection before you print =-P
▲ Page top
RE: Hide/Show
QuoteReply
# Jul 28 2003 at 1:41 PM Rating: Excellent
Illia
View User Forum
Guru
2,553 posts
Forum Preferences, under filtering settings select "No Comments"

# Jul 25 2003 at 1:23 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591789706941
43 posts
How about have the same 'Expand All/Collapse All' toggle that's on the Misty Thicket Picnic recipe? And keep the individual show/hide toggles. That way, we can collapse everything, then open only the ones we want, or vice versa.

Preference of Toggle for Hide
QuoteReply
# Jul 25 2003 at 12:20 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592248776069
6 posts
As a premium member I would like to see either hidden as the default or give us an option to let us toggle to hidden as a default. I mean I want the information, but I also like compact and easy to navigte without having to sort through information which might be chaff to me.

Superb!
QuoteReply
# Jul 24 2003 at 10:56 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1593965790794

Wow...you guys are doing excellent things! If this doesn't make people want to be premium members, nothing will!

Once again, well done folks!

This system is great and very informitive, Looks nice, and easy to navigate. The down side in my opinion is that the side link is a bit too long in my opinion (for all thats worth.) Why would ya wanna take 4 free drinks (forraged), buy a bottle, then brew to make 1 drink?
▲ Page top
RE: Why
QuoteReply
# Dec 08 2002 at 2:34 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1593362051890
View User Forum
13 posts
Another reason is a bottle only costs 5-7cp, whereas a flask of water commonly costs over 1sp each. Conversion from pod to flask also counts towards brewing trivials. Before learning of this recipe, I used to destroy alot of foraged water because I could not use it all. Now I convert it.
▲ Page top
RE: Why
QuoteReply
# Jul 23 2003 at 10:16 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591766675380
Scholar
222 posts
WHy brew it? Because for foraging classes it's a cheap way to get a few points in brewing. The same reason I turned all my foraged vegetable to vegetable oil.

For brewing points.
▲ Page top
RE: Why
QuoteReply
# Apr 22 2003 at 2:33 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591765525919
Scholar
748 posts
Flasks of water are dirt cheap anyway, so what if you saved a few copper per flask of water you brewed, it saves a lot of TIME (which is a lot more valuable) to just buy water flasks whenver you need them.
▲ Page top
RE: Why
QuoteReply
# Sep 05 2002 at 4:57 PM Rating: Default

B/c flasks of water are used in smithing, etc. Pods of water can't replace them in such recipes.
